ops.text: fli (PyPI "flights[mcp]") can never quote OKA (Okinawa Naha): in fli.models.Airport both NAH and OKA carry the value "Naha Airport", so Python's Enum collapses OKA into an alias of NAH — every `fli flights NRT OKA ...` silently queries NAH (Naha/Tahuna, Indonesia) and returns count 0 with success:true. The decode side has the same hole (_AIRPORT_BY_CODE is built by enum iteration, which skips aliases, so rows citing "OKA" raise AttributeError and get dropped). Workaround that worked: drive fli's Python API, monkeypatch FlightSearchFilters.format to rewrite the airport code "NAH"→"OKA" in the request, and add _decoders._AIRPORT_BY_CODE["OKA"] = Airport.NAH before SearchFlights().search(). Upstream fix is giving OKA a distinct enum value.
